title: Compressor
summary: General-purpose dynamics control. Automatically reduces volume when the signal exceeds the threshold. Different from Glue Compressor — use this for sidechain pumping and individual track control.
steps: Add Compressor to the track you want to compress
Set Threshold: the dB level above which compression starts. A lower threshold = more compression
Set Ratio: how much the signal is reduced above the threshold. 2:1 = gentle. 4:1 = noticeable. 8:1+ = near-limiting
Set Attack: slow attack (20–50ms) lets transients through = punchy. Fast attack (0.1–1ms) catches the transient = smooth, controlled
Set Release: how quickly the compressor recovers. Too fast = pumping/breathing. Auto = Ableton calculates the best release for the material
For sidechain: expand Sidechain section, enable, set Audio From to your kick track. Now the compressor ducks when the kick hits
Watch the gain reduction meter — aim for 2–6dB of reduction on the peaks
tips: Slow attack = more punch (the transient gets through before the compressor reacts). This is counterintuitive but correct
For the sub bass sidechain: Ratio 6:1, Attack 0.5ms, Release 100ms. For pad sidechain: Ratio 4:1, Attack 2ms, Release 200ms
Knee setting: Hard knee = compressor kicks in abruptly at the threshold. Soft knee = gradual transition around the threshold. Soft knee sounds more natural
Never use a Compressor on the Master — use Limiter for the master output ceiling. Compressor on master kills dynamics in ways that are hard to undo

images.caption: Drag the Threshold knob left until the GR bar (gain reduction — the vertical bar on the right) shows 2–6dB of movement on peaks. Set Ratio, Attack, and Release in the center panel. For sidechain: click the 'Sidechain' triangle at top-right, enable it, then set 'Audio From' to your kick source.

ableton_version_note: Available in all editions of Live 9+.
